editableNewDialogue.text: For the Amazon Kindle listing, should the categories lean more toward Science Fiction (space adventure) or more Fantasy (mythic, magical tone)?

It depends on which elements dominate on-page. If the story focuses on spacecraft, observatories, signals, and exploration, go Science Fiction > Space Exploration/Adventure. If it leans into cosmic spirits, prophecies, or overt magic, go Fantasy > Epic/Myths & Legends. A common approach is to pick one primary category for discoverability, then use keywords to capture the crossover audience.
Can you give 10 category paths for each approach, plus a short 3–4 line ad blurb?

Sure—here are two sets of 10 category paths (pick the closest matches available in KDP), plus two ad blurbs.

SCI-FI-LEANING (Space Adventure)
1) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>Space Exploration
2) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>Adventure
3) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>Space Opera
4) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>First Contact
5) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>Alien Invasion
6) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>Hard Science Fiction
7) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>Technothrillers
8) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Teen & Young Adult>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>Space Opera
9) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Teen & Young Adult>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Science Fiction>Adventure
10) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Literature & Fiction>Action & Adventure>Science Fiction

FANTASY-LEANING (Mythic Cosmic)
1) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Epic
2) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Myths & Legends
3) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Action & Adventure
4) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Coming of Age
5) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Paranormal & Urban
6) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Sword & Sorcery
7) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Teen & Young Adult>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Epic
8) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Teen & Young Adult>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Myths & Legends
9) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Children’s eBooks>Fairy Tales, Folk Tales & Myths>Myths & Legends
10) Kindle Store>Kindle eBooks>Science Fiction & Fantasy>Fantasy>Dragons & Mythical Creatures

AD BLURB (Sci-Fi)
A strange signal. A blazing comet. A mission that rewrites humanity’s place in the universe. Chase Comet Zenith across the dark between worlds—and uncover a legacy meant for all starfarers.

AD BLURB (Fantasy)
A comet’s tail carries more than ice and fire—it carries a message. Follow brave chasers into a sky of omens, ancient symbols, and cosmic destiny. The stars are calling, and the secret is alive.
Also, what’s a good keyword set that avoids being too generic?

Aim for specific “story signals” (artifact + comet + ancient map) rather than broad terms like “space” or “fantasy.” Examples:
- comet signal mystery
- ancient alien obelisk
- deep space chase
- cosmic artifact discovery
- interstellar coordinates
- astronaut teen adventure
- space anomalies creatures
- galactic hologram map
- first contact mystery
- space exploration team

Before I draft the 10 Kindle category paths and the short preface/exit page for *Comet Chasers*, do you want them aimed at **Middle Grade** or **Teen & Young Adult** shelves? That choice changes the best-fit browsing paths and keywords.
Teen & Young Adult—keep it adventurous, not too childish.
Stop doing exactly two scenes every chapter. For the next outlines, I want three scenes per chapter (still include an image prompt for every scene).
Got it—moving forward I’ll write each chapter with **three scenes per chapter**, and I’ll include an **image prompt for every scene**.
